learning_outcomes:
1 1.  |  Major Programme:
Upon completion of their studies, students
will have
(1)  |  a critical
understanding and appreciation of the interpretation of the Bible and
Christian faith (K/S/V);
(2)  |  knowledge and
appreciation of Christian literature (K/S/V);
(3)  |  understanding of the
correlation between application of Christian belief and contemporary life
contexts (S/V);
(4)  |  a self-reflective
attitude on student’s own belief and value (S/V); and
(5)  |  a foundation and
life-long interest in learning the knowledge of Christianity (K/S/V).
K = Knowledge outcomes; S = Skills
outcomes; V = Value and attitude outcomes
2.  |  Minor Programme:
Upon the completion of the Minor Programme,
students will have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental issues
related to the subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject
with sufficient exposure and at a suitable level to allow further study in
this subject or related subjects or to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Theology Theology Applicable to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 70 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
Any three courses from:
ANTH1020, ARTS1004, 1005, 1010 BMBL1001,
CHES1100, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000, 1110, 1400[CUMT1000], ENGE1000,
FAAS1900, HIST1000, 1700 or 1701, JASP1090, LING1000, MUSC1000, THEO1000,
TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  28
CURE1122, PHIL1110, THEO1211, 1212,
2235, 2236, 2241, 3221
Capstone Courses: THEO4241, 4242
3.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  One course from each of Theological
Studies Areas 2, 4 and 5  |  9
(b)  |  At least two courses at 2000 or
above level from each of Theological Studies Areas 1 and 3  |  12
(c)  |  12 units of elective courses chosen
from the following:  |  12
All THEO courses other than those
taken in 2, 3(a) and (b) above, and all CURE courses except those listed under
Cultural Studies Areas
Total:  |  70
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  THEO and CURE courses at 2000 and
above level will be included in the calculation of the Major GPA for
honours classification.
2.  |  Students in their final year of
attendance are allowed to take THEO and CURE courses at 5000 or above
level, subject to the approval of the Director of Divinity School of
Chung Chi College and the Chairman of the Department of Cultural and
Religious Studies.

Minor Programme Title Theology
Minor Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 18 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Elective Courses:  |  18
6 THEO courses with at least 1
course at 2000 or above level and another 2 courses at 3000 or above
level.
Total:  |  18
